Identity.History: Samudragupta ruled the Gupta Empire around 335–375 AD and transformed it into a major imperial power. Son of Chandragupta I, he was a brilliant conqueror whose campaigns across northern and central India are recorded in the Allahabad Pillar inscription. He defeated many kings, annexed some territories, and made others tributaries, creating a flexible imperial network. Also praised as a poet, musician, and patron of Brahmanical religion, he issued distinctive gold coins celebrating his power and culture. His reign marked the rise of Gupta supremacy in India.
